subPoints: Nice idea to provide default value for some of the parameters.
Good approach converting input to uppercase and then replacing `T` with `U`.
Good input validation: raising `TypeError` for non-string input.
Good input validation: raising `ValueError` for invalid nucleotides.
Very good job on incorporating the unit test concepts into your user-defined function development.
For `PyTest` - good use of `pytest.raises(ValueError)` to verify that invalid characters raise an error in the most concise manner.
For `unittest` - good use of `with self.assertRaises(ValueError)` to verify that invalid characters raise an error in the most concise manner.
Good job at developing an alternative approach (e.g., usig a boolean flag or unittest's `self.fail`).
Good use of random module (e.g., `random.randint`, `random.choices`) to create randomized toy data.
Interesting use of the following random functions:
Very good validation of a length arguments (e.g., negative lengths, max and/or min lengths) for safe toy data generation.
Good job on using the PyTest module/library.
Good job on using the unittest module/library.
You created a good transcription user-defined function.
Your unit tests correctly check your function.
Tests are readable and follow a logical naming scheme.
Toy data - consider including a validation for invalid ranges (e.g., negative sequence lengths).
Consider including testing edge cases (e.g., `TTTT`, `CtGg`, `GAGA`).
Did not correctly implement the transcription rule DNA to RNA by replacing all `T`s with `U`s. You needed to use `str.replace().`
Did not handle case-insensitivity - failed to convert lowercase input to uppercase before validation/transcription). Please see Python's `str.upper()` function.
Allowed invalid characters without raising `ValueError`.
Missing at least one required tests (i.e., standard transcription, case-insensitivity, and/or invalid-character `ValueError`).
Exception test doesn't work correctly (e.g., the call to `your_function(...)` not placed inside the `assertRaises` context).
Multiple tests are joined together, rather than having specific idea isolation localized to specific tests.
One or more of your developed tests are failing.
Tests rely on `print()` statements instead of assertions.
Generated sequences contain characters outside `{A, T, C, G}`.
Did not randomize sequence length (e.g., using `random.randint)`.
Did not randomize the sequence (e.g., using `random.choices()`).
